background: The probability of a defective unit in a manufacturing process is q = 0.06. That means, the probability of a successful unit is p = 0.94. Using the normal approximation, obtain the probability that the number of defective units in a sample of 1000 units is between 50 and 70.

task.query: Using the normal approximation, obtain the probability that the number of defective units in a sample of 1000 units is between 50 and 70.
task.answer:
The probability that the number of defective units in a sample of 1000 units is between 50 and 70 is given by using the normal approximation:

$$
\begin{aligned}
P(50 \leq X \leq 70) & \simeq \Phi\left(\frac{70.5-60}{\sqrt{56.4}}\right)-\Phi\left(\frac{49.5-60}{\sqrt{56.4}}\right) \text { as } n q=60 \text { is large } \\
& =0.8379
\end{aligned}
$$

In general, for $\mathrm{a}<\mathrm{b}$ we can obtain

$$
P(a \leq X \leq b) \simeq \Phi\left(\frac{b+1 / 2-n p}{\sqrt{n p(1-p)}}\right)-\Phi\left(\frac{a-1 / 2-n p}{\sqrt{n p(1-p)}}\right)
$$

where $X$ is binomial $\mathrm{b}(n, p)$ with the expected of $X$ is $n p$ for large $n$.
task.method: 事件发生概率及独立性
